g g g

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check the current right panel state
nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
blocks = [n for n in nodes if n['pixels'] == 9]
grid = {}
for b in blocks:
    r = round((b['boundary'][0][0] - 49) / 5)
    c = round((b['boundary'][0][1] - 24) / 5)
    grid[(r, c)] = b['color']
print("Right panel:")
for r in range(3):
    print(f"  {[grid.get((r, c), '?') for c in range(3)]}")

# All cells are gray. This is not the target pattern.
# Let me think about what the target pattern might be.
# The left panel has purple at cross-edge and gray at corners/center.
# Maybe the target pattern is to have all cells gray? But that doesn't seem right.
# Let me try pressing RIGHT to reset the panel and try a different approach.
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

white gray white
gray white gray
```
Let me try pressing RIGHT to reset the panel, and then clicking on the cross-edge cells to toggle them from white to green, and then clicking on the corner/center cells to toggle them from green to gray.

Let me try clicking on the left panel's purple dots to see if it toggles the right panel's cells.
